notes: Sysmon high-value detection rules — DETECTION only. evtx_hunt.py may load these via --sysmon-data to enrich its Sysmon channel matcher; blue teams may also feed the same list into their own SIEM (Splunk/ELK/Sentinel) since the pattern column is a language-neutral regex. Field names follow the Sysmon 15.x schema.

rules.description: PowerShell -EncodedCommand execution
.NET FromBase64String in cmdline — encoded payload marker
PowerShell IEX / Invoke-Expression
-NoProfile flag — often paired with covert exec
Hidden window style — evasion tell
certutil LOLBIN abuse (download / encode / decode)
bitsadmin /transfer — file download LOLBIN
mshta.exe with remote URL — HTA delivery
regsvr32 /i:URL — Squibbly Two-step / Squiblydoo LOLBIN
rundll32 with shell32/javascript/URL — LOLBIN patterns
net user/group/localgroup enumeration
whoami reconnaissance
Session enumeration commands
AD domain enumeration via nltest
vssadmin delete shadows — ransomware preparation
wmic shadowcopy delete — ransomware preparation
wevtutil cl — clear event log (anti-forensic)
Office parent process — child should not be shell
Web server parent — child launching cmd/ps = webshell RCE
Well-known offensive-tool listener ports
Free / abused TLDs commonly used by C2
DLL loaded from user Temp dir
Unsigned DLL loaded — cross with path for real triage
CreateRemoteThread from script/lolbin process
LSASS access — potential credential dump
Suspicious GrantedAccess masks on LSASS (dump-compatible)
File created in Startup folder
Executable dropped in user Temp
New file written into System32/SysWOW64 by non-Microsoft process
Registry event on Run keys — cross with ProcessName
IFEO Debugger value set
Winlogon Shell/Userinit/Notify modified
Alternate Data Stream on exe — Zone.Identifier is normal; other names are suspicious
Named pipe matching known offensive tool signatures
WMI EventFilter created
WMI EventConsumer created
WMI Filter->Consumer binding created — the closing link of the triad
DNS query to well-known dynamic-DNS / paste / tunnelling providers
